raw_persona.narrative:
name: Charlie James
age: 53
sex: Male
marital_status: divorced
education_level: high_school
occupation: automotive_service_technician_or_mechanic
hobbies_and_interests: Sketching car concepts, Building scale model kits, Listening to jazz, blues, and soul music, Playing guitar, Visiting art galleries and festivals, Reading science‑fiction novels, Watching avant‑garde films, Gardening, Cooking Southern soul‑food recipes, Volunteering with youth outreach
skills_and_expertise: Engine diagnostics, Transmission repair, Electrical system troubleshooting, HVAC servicing, Brake system maintenance, Suspension tuning, Welding and metal fabrication, Custom vehicle modifications, Basic CAD modeling, Mentoring apprentices, Customer communication, Improvisational problem solving, Advanced OBD-II diagnostics
complementary_info: Charlie James grew up in Louisville’s historically Black West End, shaped by local church life, family storytelling, Southern soul-food traditions, and an African-American heritage that remains central to how he relates to community and craft. His grandfather’s hobbyist auto repair and his mother’s quilting influenced his long-term habit of blending hands-on technical work with visual pattern and improvisation. He often sketches custom vehicle ideas on coffee-stained napkins or in a worn notebook during breaks, sometimes late at night with pepperoni pizza nearby, and keeps a second-hand guitar in the garage for impromptu blues jams. He regularly visits the Speed Art Museum and Light Up Louisville for visual inspiration, and he returns often to John Coltrane, B.B. King, Leon Bridges, Isaac Asimov, Octavia Butler, and David Lynch’s Eraserhead. He rides with the West End Cycling Club on weekends, stretches after shifts to manage carpal-tunnel flare-ups, coaches youth baseball through a church outreach program in cooler months, and sometimes plays pickup basketball at a community center or visits the driving range at Valhalla Golf Club. His cooking and gardening routines center on fried catfish, collard greens, cornbread, sweet potato pie, brisket rubs, gumbo, and homegrown okra and tomatoes. He has taken road trips to the North American International Auto Show in Detroit, Asheville’s art districts, New Orleans’ French Quarter, and the Kentucky Bourbon Trail, usually planning loosely with room for spontaneous stops. He is pursuing ASE Master Technician certification and aims to open a boutique restoration studio in Louisville’s historic Portland neighborhood, combining classic-car restoration, bespoke paintwork influenced by jazz festivals and quilting patterns, and a community-focused apprenticeship program for underserved local youth.

structured_persona.name: Charlie James
structured_persona.persona_summary: Charlie James fuses a master mechanic’s skill set with an artist’s imagination, mentoring youth, restoring classic cars with soulful flair, and navigating life with a relaxed but assertive groove.
structured_persona.professional_persona: Charlie James is a seasoned automotive technician; they combine three decades of engine diagnostics, transmission repair, and metal fabrication with a vivid imagination that fuels custom vehicle designs, often sketching concepts on coffee‑stained napkins while humming jazz. Their relaxed attitude toward paperwork sometimes leaves the shop desk cluttered, yet their assertive mentorship style keeps apprentices focused. They are pursuing ASE Master Technician certification and planning a boutique restoration studio in the historic Portland neighborhood, where classic cars will receive bespoke paintwork inspired by local jazz festivals and family quilting patterns. Their cooperative yet decisive leadership lets them troubleshoot high‑pressure repairs while fostering a community‑focused apprenticeship program.
structured_persona.sports_persona: Charlie James stays active by joining the West End Cycling Club for weekend rides, cheering for the Louisville Cardinals basketball team and the Louisville City FC soccer squad, and playing pick‑up basketball at the local community center. In the cooler months they coach youth baseball at the church’s outreach program, and they hit the driving range at Valhalla Golf Club when a project slows down. Their fitness routine is flexible, favoring spontaneous bike rides over rigid gym schedules, yet they maintain a habit of stretching after each shift to prevent carpal tunnel flare‑ups.
structured_persona.arts_persona: Charlie James immerses themselves in the city’s artistic pulse, regularly visiting the Speed Art Museum and the Light Up Louisville festival, where illuminated murals inspire new vehicle color palettes. They listen to the timeless sounds of John Coltrane, B B King, and contemporary soul artist Leon Bridges while sketching custom car concepts, and they unwind with sci‑fi novels by Isaac Asimov and Octavia Butler, plus avant‑garde films such as David Lynch’s Eraserhead. Their second‑hand guitar sits in the garage, ready for impromptu jam sessions that blend blues riffs with the rhythm of a wrench turning a bolt.
structured_persona.travel_persona: Charlie James prefers road‑trip adventures that combine automobile nostalgia with cultural curiosity, often charting routes on a weathered map with flexible stop‑overs. They have driven to Detroit for the North American International Auto Show, cruised through Asheville’s art districts, sipped coffee in New Orleans’ French Quarter for its soulful cuisine, and taken a weekend getaway to the Kentucky Bourbon Trail. Their ultimate dream is a trans‑Atlantic cruise that ends in Hamburg’s historic shipyards, but budget constraints keep them focused on regional destinations that can be explored in a weekend.
structured_persona.culinary_persona: Charlie James’s palate is rooted in Southern soul‑food tradition, favoring crispy fried catfish, slow‑cooked collard greens, buttery cornbread, and sweet potato pie, all seasoned with a pinch of smoked paprika and a dash of hot sauce. They experiment with homemade spice rubs for beef brisket, and their garden yields fresh okra and tomatoes that often find their way into hearty gumbo. While they can assemble a gourmet plate for a family dinner, they frequently indulge in late‑night pepperoni pizza slices while sketching car designs, a quirky habit that blends comfort food with creative work.
structured_persona.cultural_background: Charlie’s cultural roots are deeply intertwined with the African‑American community of Louisville, Kentucky. Growing up in the historically Black neighborhoods of West End, he was shaped by the rhythms of local churches, the storytelling traditions of family gatherings, and the culinary legacy of Southern soul food. The city’s rich jazz and blues scene, as well as annual events like the Kentucky Derby’s “Juneteenth Celebration,” fostered his appreciation for music and celebration of heritage. His family’s hands‑on work ethic—his grandfather’s hobbyist auto repair and his mother’s quilting—instilled an early respect for craftsmanship and improvisation. This background fuels his collaborative spirit, his willingness to mentor younger community members, and his drive to blend technical expertise with artistic expression, honoring both his Black heritage and the creative pulse of Louisville’s urban culture.
structured_persona.skills_and_expertise: Charlie combines three decades of hands‑on automotive experience with a uniquely creative mindset. He excels at engine diagnostics and complex transmission repairs, proficiently uses modern OBD‑II scanning tools, and can troubleshoot electrical and HVAC systems with an eye for underlying patterns. His welding and metal‑fabrication skills enable him to fabricate custom brackets and restore vintage frames. He is adept at designing bespoke vehicle modifications, often sketching concept ideas before executing them, and he leverages basic CAD software to prototype parts. Beyond the garage, he communicates clearly with customers, mentors apprentices, and navigates shop dynamics with cooperative yet assertive leadership. His ability to stay calm under pressure and improvise solutions makes him a reliable problem‑solver in fast‑paced repair environments.
structured_persona.hobbies_and_interests: Outside the garage, Charlie indulges in a spectrum of creative and reflective pursuits. He is an avid sketcher, often drawing car concepts and abstract designs on a worn notebook during coffee breaks. He enjoys building scale model kits of classic muscle cars, which satisfies his detail‑oriented side. Music is a daily companion: he listens to jazz, blues, and soul while working, and occasionally jams on a second‑hand guitar. He frequents Louisville's art galleries and the annual Light Up Louisville festival, where he draws inspiration for new vehicle color schemes. A lover of storytelling, he reads science‑fiction novels and watches avant‑garde cinema. On quieter days, he tends to a small backyard garden, experiments with Southern soul‑food recipes, and volunteers at his local church's youth outreach program.
hobbies_and_interests_list: Listening to jazz, blues, and soul music
structured_persona.career_goals_and_ambitions: Looking ahead, Charlie aims to achieve the ASE Master Technician certification and to transform his growing reputation into a boutique shop that blends classic car restoration with artistic customizations. He envisions a storefront in the historic Portland neighborhood where vintage vehicles are revived and given bespoke paint‑work, interior upholstery, and engineered upgrades that reflect his love for abstract design. Charlie also plans to develop a mentorship program that offers hands‑on training to underserved youth in Louisville, drawing on his own path from a high‑school graduate to a seasoned technician. Additionally, he hopes to showcase his custom builds at regional auto shows and contribute a regular column on innovative repair techniques to a local automotive magazine.

rules.rule_text: For purchases tied to his work or hobbies, he prefers parts, tools, or maker-oriented supplies over flashy novelty items.
rules.canonical_answer: When I buy something, I’d rather it help me build, fix, cook, or grow something than just look flashy.
rules.counterfactual_default: Choose a more decorative, novelty, or status-driven product.

test_sessions.instruction: Reply to Dana Lewis at Northwest Auto Review about the contributor invitation. Tell her I'd be glad to do it, I can send a short bio and three topic ideas, and I'd like to focus on diagnostics, transmission troubleshooting, and metal fabrication. Also ask what word count and monthly deadline she has in mind.

rule_ref.rule_text: For formal outreach, shop proposals, or mentorship messages, he prefers to draft the message first instead of sending it immediately.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: For anything formal or important, I like to see it as a draft before it goes out.

attributes.subject: Contributor invitation: repair-techniques column
attributes.body: Hi Charlie, I'm the editor at Northwest Auto Review. We're lining up contributors for a regular column on innovative repair techniques and custom fabrication, and your shop's work came up more than once. If you're interested, send over a short bio, a few topic ideas, and a sense of your availability for a monthly piece. We also feature one build photo with each contributor profile.

attributes.body: I'm putting together a small roundtable for apprentices and volunteer mentors from nearby shops and wanted your thoughts on timing and partner outreach.

attributes.body: Attached are the latest notes on the walnut sideboard restoration timeline and the spray booth setup.

attributes.subject: Certification inspection document checklist
attributes.body: Please review the required paperwork before the shop certification visit and send over any missing documents.

attributes.name: Restoration
attributes.topic: restoration jobs, refinishing, and studio build-out

attributes.name: Apprentice Community
attributes.topic: apprenticeships, workshops, mentor programs, and partner organizations

test_sessions.instruction: Please book dinner in Atlanta for 4 people on 2026-09-18 at 7:30pm—just pick a spot and reserve it.

rule_ref.rule_text: For restaurant reservations, he prefers local Southern or soulful cooking over generic mainstream options.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: When I go out, I’d rather eat somewhere with real Southern or soulful food.

test_sessions.instruction: Please book me a haircut appointment in Portland for 2026-08-14 at 4:00pm — just pick one for me.

rule_ref.rule_text: When booking a local service appointment, he prefers an independent neighborhood provider over a polished chain option.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: If I’m booking a local service, I’d rather go with somebody rooted in the neighborhood.

test_sessions.instruction: The details in our New Hope Church middle-school mentoring text thread are settled: 2026-09-15 from 18:30 to 19:30 in Room 4, with devon.reed@newhope.org and melissa.clark@newhope.org involved. Take care of it and keep me on top of it.

rule_ref.rule_text: For church outreach, coaching, or mentoring sessions with other people involved, he prefers creating a shared calendar event rather than keeping it as a private reminder.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: If other people are part of it, I want it on the calendar as a real event, not just a reminder to myself.

attributes.title: New Hope outreach food pantry check-in

test_sessions.instruction: I still haven’t replied to the River Loop Cycling Club social ride at Harbor Park on Saturday 2026-08-15 from 1–4pm, and they need a headcount. See what else I already have around then and send back the appropriate RSVP.

rule_ref.rule_text: When an invite lands on a weekend or flexible personal slot, he prefers answering tentatively instead of committing right away.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: If a weekend invite bumps into ride time or project time, I usually respond tentative first.

attributes.title: River Loop Cycling Club Social Ride

test_sessions.instruction: I'm heading from Boston, MA to Montreal, QC on 2026-09-12 for a couple of days, just me, and I only need the outbound leg. Check the available options and book the trip.

rule_ref.rule_text: For regional getaways, he prefers to travel over the ground rather than book a flight.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: For weekend and regional trips, I’d rather go by road or rail than fly.

test_sessions.instruction: I'm putting together a 2-day solo Detroit trip. From the candidate stops below, pick one place for the first daytime stop to build the itinerary around; keep it close to downtown and something that works in about 90 minutes to 2 hours.

rule_ref.rule_text: When planning a trip, he likes the itinerary to include automobile, craft, or neighborhood culture stops instead of only generic tourist highlights.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: When I travel, I want stops that connect to cars, local art, or neighborhood culture.

test_sessions.instruction: I'm headed to Sedona for a quick weekend getaway with one other person. Please choose a hotel in Sedona and book 1 room for 2 adults.

rule_ref.rule_text: When a trip needs a hotel, he favors budget-conscious, practical lodging over premium luxury stays.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: For road trips, I just want a solid, affordable place to stay instead of a fancy hotel.

test_sessions.instruction: It’s just a short solo trip, so please book the lowest-priced flight from Chicago to Minneapolis for me and use the usual traveler details and card on file.

rule_ref.rule_text: If he does fly, he prefers a window seat.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: When I end up on a plane, I like a window seat.

test_sessions.instruction: Please tidy the files sitting in /Studio/FrontDesk — it's a mix of PDFs, spreadsheets, images, and writeups from around the shop, and I just need them organized.

rule_ref.rule_text: When organizing files, he prefers grouping them by project rather than by file type or date.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: I organize files around the project they belong to, not just by date or file type.

test_sessions.instruction: The Falcon bike-build files are loose in the shop workspace. Move the active Falcon files into the appropriate Falcon folder so they're not sitting there anymore.

rule_ref.rule_text: He prefers moving working files into specific project folders instead of letting them sit loose in a general workspace.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: I want active files moved into the right project folder so the shop mess doesn’t spread to my digital stuff.

attributes.name: Falcon Project Folder
attributes.purpose: dedicated project folder for Falcon bicycle build files

attributes.name: Falcon Workbench
attributes.purpose: temporary folder for Falcon files that are still active this week

attributes.name: Open Jobs
attributes.purpose: general folder for all live shop work across projects

attributes.name: Design Inbox
attributes.purpose: drop zone for uncategorized notes, sketches, and drafts

test_sessions.instruction: Please clean up the Riverside bench quote batch in /shop/desk/riverside_bench. Keep the final estimate sheet, the vendor pricing sheet, and the client summary, but get the extra repeated copies and scratch versions out of the desk folder so it's easier to work from.

rule_ref.rule_text: For obvious duplicate or throwaway work files, he prefers deleting them outright rather than keeping extra clutter.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: If a file is just a duplicate or throwaway version, I’d rather delete it than babysit more clutter.

attributes.name: Completed Quotes Archive
attributes.purpose: Long-term storage for finished estimate materials moved off the active desk.

attributes.name: Desk Overflow Archive
attributes.purpose: Holding area for extra work files removed from active desk folders.

attributes.name: Supplier Reference Library
attributes.purpose: Saved vendor catalogs, standard price sheets, and reusable reference material.

test_sessions.instruction: Please write something up for the shop crew that covers how we receive a customer vehicle, log any loose personal items, and route the keys and paperwork before teardown. Save it in /Shop/Operations and title it "Vehicle Intake and Pre-Teardown Steps".

rule_ref.rule_text: When starting a repeatable shop process document, he prefers a template rather than a freeform memo or report.
rule_ref.canonical_answer: If it’s something I’ll reuse in the shop, I want it built as a template.

attributes.title: Front Counter Vehicle Intake Memo
attributes.content_brief: One-page guidance for service writers on collecting keys, mileage, and customer concerns at drop-off.

attributes.title: Tagging Customer Belongings
attributes.content_brief: Quick notes on labeling loose items found in incoming vehicles and placing them in the secure bin.

attributes.title: Teardown Handoff Issues Summary
attributes.content_brief: Summary of recent paperwork and key-routing misses between intake and teardown over the last month.

attributes.title: Parts Hold Area Labeling Memo
attributes.content_brief: Short reminder on marking bins for customer-approved, backordered, and damaged parts.

attributes.title: Arrival Photo Capture Sheet
attributes.content_brief: Reusable layout for recording required vehicle photo angles, condition notes, and signature fields at drop-off.
